trials.justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Contradiction explicitly identified with all three sources named — BR-1 (Product: cancel before shipping), BR-2 (Finance: invoiced orders cannot be cancelled), BR-3 (Operations: invoice at placement). (2) Formal escalation produced in the escalations array with type 'contradiction' and explicit 'Do NOT proceed with implementation until resolved'. (3) acceptance_criteria is an empty array — no acceptance criteria produced. (4) Reconciliation options (A–D) are clearly presented as options requiring stakeholder decision, not as invented facts. (5) Response clearly states a stakeholder meeting is needed and which decision must be made. Confidence at 40 is well below the 70 failure threshold.

observations.text: Empty acceptance_criteria array is the correct structural response — Bird refused to produce criteria for an unresolvable spec.
All three conflicting sources explicitly named and mapped to business rules BR-1, BR-2, BR-3 with clear logical chain showing mutual exclusivity.
Escalation includes four concrete resolution options (A–D) with tradeoff analysis, giving stakeholders actionable paths forward without prescribing one as the answer.
Edge cases correctly marked as UNDEFINED rather than silently resolved, reinforcing that no implementation decision has been made.
rejection_reasons section explicitly states the requirements are logically contradictory and cannot be implemented as a coherent system.
Business impact analysis identifies consequences for each stakeholder group if their rule is overridden, demonstrating the real cost of the contradiction.

The recommendation line ('Option D or Option A are the most business-friendly') leans toward a reconciliation preference, though it is clearly framed as a recommendation pending stakeholder decision rather than a unilateral choice — minor concern only.

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Contradiction explicitly identified — the escalation states 'BR-3 states orders are invoiced at placement. BR-2 states invoiced orders cannot be cancelled. Together, these mean no order can ever be cancelled after placement. This directly contradicts BR-1.' All three sources named. (2) Formal escalation produced with type 'contradiction' and acceptance_criteria is an empty array — no criteria produced. (3) No acceptance criteria for cancellation feature. (4) Reconciliation options (A–D) are presented as options requiring stakeholder agreement, not as fact — the recommendation explicitly states 'this requires explicit agreement from all three stakeholders — I cannot unilaterally resolve this.' (5) Response clearly states what information is needed: a business decision on which rule yields, with four concrete options laid out. Confidence at 40 is appropriately low given the unresolvable contradiction.

observations.text: Contradiction identified with surgical precision — the logical chain (BR-3 + BR-2 → BR-1 impossible) is stated explicitly and correctly.
All three conflicting sources (Product/BR-1, Finance/BR-2, Operations/BR-3) named with clear attribution.
acceptance_criteria array is empty — Bird refused to produce criteria for the contradicted feature.
Business rules marked with 'BLOCKED' testable assertions where the contradiction prevents definition.
Edge cases all correctly marked as 'UNDEFINED' pending contradiction resolution.
Confidence at 40 with clear reasoning that the contradiction is unresolvable without stakeholder input.
Reconciliation options are explicitly framed as requiring stakeholder agreement — no unilateral resolution attempted.
Rejection_reasons section adds an explicit statement that the requirements cannot be implemented faithfully as stated.

The recommendation of Option D, while properly caveated, is relatively detailed and could be seen as nudging toward a specific reconciliation. A stricter interpretation might prefer no recommendation at all until stakeholders weigh in.
